response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine distributed systems depth (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic), current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, and a long track record of Staff-level technical breadth across many stacks. However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from both the skills section and all work history, which is a material gap for this role. Stakeholder management and cross-functional coordination are not evidenced in the resume. The current Staff title is only ~1 month old, limiting signal on strategic impact at that level. Worth a conversation to probe the Python gap and stakeholder experience before deciding.
strengths: Current role involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, directly aligned with the AI-native engineering environment the role expects as a baseline
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby — micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design
Long career with genuine technical breadth (web, embedded, GIS, mobile, DevOps) and recurring Staff/tech-lead designations indicating systems thinking at scale
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills list and every work history entry — it is listed as a must-have strong skill for this role
No explicit evidence of stakeholder management, cross-functional coordination with product or leadership, or operating in a multi-team technical strategy capacity
Current Staff Engineer tenure at re:cinq is approximately one month, providing insufficient signal on strategic impact or ownership at that seniority level
